title: City of Seattle - Renting in Seattle
description: A comprehensive web portal for Seattle renters and landlords, designed in compliance with the City's style guide to provide a 360-degree view of the rental process.
caseStudyWhat: The Seattle Department of Construction & Inspections (SDCI) engaged the creative studio I support to architect and design a web portal, which would live within SDCI's existing architecture and website. The portal provides Seattle renters and landlords with a comprehensive 360-degree view of the rental process. In compliance with the City's new style guide, the illustrator and web designer delivered a set of intuitive wireframes and redline designs that were built by the City's development team.
caseStudyHow: Our UX team worked with stakeholders from City of Seattle and internal agency account managers to learn as much as possible about the target demographic and current userbase of SDCI; Utilizing data, user studies, and stakeholder interviews.
caseStudyResults: The City of Seattle gained a new resource for tenants and landlords that offers concise and accessible information about tenant rights and policies around renting and leasing property.
caseStudyRole: Led a team of UX specialists to build out a set user-centered wireframes for client review and acceptance
Collaborated with city officials and our internal graphic design team to build full resolution design mockups that are optimized for implementation by the City of Seattle's web development team
QA and testing for accessibility and cross-browser compatibility
